candidate_sources_rejected: NICE treatment recommendations for nail psoriasis
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: No topical, systemic, biologic, medicine, dose, monitoring, referral, or treatment recommendation was mapped.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults, young people, or children with clinician-established or suspected psoriasis affecting nails; fungal, trauma, medicine, and joint contexts require separate assessment.
setting_applicability: UAE primary and specialist dermatology or rheumatology review.
UAE_applicability: NICE assessment fields are transferable, but UAE dermatology, rheumatology, fungal-testing, medicine, monitoring, and referral pathways remain required.
recency_verification: NICE CG153, last updated in 2017, was reviewed on 2026-07-14; material recency uncertainty remains.
superseded_check: NICE CG153 remains the current official psoriasis guideline identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact psoriasis assessment section explicitly includes nail involvement and supports documenting duration, interval change, symptoms, functional or quality-of-life impact, distribution and clinician-observed nail or skin findings without inferring diagnosis or treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting psoriasis type, duration, extent, distribution, nails, high-impact sites, symptoms, systemic upset, daily-life impact, and wellbeing without inferring an individual diagnosis or treatment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Joint-symptom and clinician-led referral qualifiers were reviewed, but no arthritis diagnosis or referral is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports asking about joint symptoms and recording clinician-led specialist assessment or referral only when completed or arranged.

unresolved_source_gaps: Specific nail signs and count, skin psoriasis extent, joint symptoms, fungal and trauma differential, diagnosis, severity score, investigations, treatment, monitoring, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
